questions.text: The collection of all points in a plane, which are at a fixed distance from a fixed point in the plane, is called a:
questions.explanation: This is the formal definition of a circle.

questions.text: The fixed point in the definition of a circle is called the:
questions.explanation: All points on the circle are equidistant from the center.

questions.text: A line segment joining the center and any point on the circle is called:
questions.explanation: The radius is the constant distance from the center to the boundary.

questions.text: The longest chord of a circle is its:
questions.explanation: The diameter is a chord that passes through the center and is twice the radius.

questions.text: A circle divides the plane on which it lies into ______ parts.
questions.explanation: The parts are the interior, the circle itself, and the exterior.

questions.text: A piece of a circle between two points is called an:
questions.explanation: An arc is a continuous portion of the circumference.

questions.text: Equal chords of a circle subtend ______ angles at the center.
questions.explanation: This is a fundamental theorem of circles; triangles formed are congruent.

questions.text: The perpendicular from the center of a circle to a chord:
questions.explanation: The line from the center perpendicular to a chord divides it into two equal halves.

questions.text: There is one and only one circle passing through ______ given non-collinear points.
questions.explanation: Three non-collinear points uniquely determine a circle (the circumcircle of the triangle they form).

questions.text: Equal chords of a circle are ______ from the center.
questions.explanation: Chords of equal length are located at the same perpendicular distance from the center.

questions.text: The angle subtended by an arc at the center is ______ the angle subtended by it at any point on the remaining part of the circle.
questions.explanation: This is a key theorem: Angle at center = 2 × Angle at circumference.

questions.text: Angles in the same segment of a circle are:
questions.explanation: Any two angles subtended by the same arc on the same side of the chord are equal.

questions.text: An angle in a semi-circle is a:
questions.explanation: The angle subtended by the diameter at any point on the circle is always 90°.

questions.text: A quadrilateral is called cyclic if all its four vertices lie on a:
questions.explanation: Cyclic quadrilaterals have vertices that are concyclic.

questions.text: The sum of either pair of opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral is:
questions.explanation: Opposite angles in a cyclic quadrilateral are supplementary.

questions.text: If the sum of a pair of opposite angles of a quadrilateral is 180°, the quadrilateral is:
questions.explanation: This is the converse of the cyclic quadrilateral theorem.

questions.text: The region between a chord and either of its arcs is called a:
questions.explanation: A chord divides the circle into two segments: major and minor.

questions.text: The region between an arc and the two radii joining its endpoints to the center is called a:
questions.explanation: Sectors are pie-shaped regions of a circle.

questions.text: A line which intersects a circle at two points is called a:
questions.explanation: Unlike a chord which is a segment, a secant is an infinite line passing through two points of the circle.

questions.text: Length of a circle is its:
questions.explanation: Circumference is the distance around the circle.

questions.text: The radius of a circle is 5 cm. The length of the longest chord is:
questions.explanation: Longest chord is diameter = 2 × radius = 10 cm.

questions.text: Two circles are congruent if they have the same:
questions.explanation: Circles are identical if their radii are equal.

questions.text: If two arcs of a circle are congruent, then their corresponding chords are:
questions.explanation: Congruent arcs have the same measure and thus produce equal chords.

questions.text: The center of a circle lies in ______ of the circle.
questions.explanation: The center is the point from which the circle is generated, located inside.

questions.text: If AB is a chord and O is center, and OM is perpendicular to AB, then AM is ______ MB.
questions.explanation: Perpendicular from center bisects the chord.

questions.text: How many circles can pass through one given point?
questions.explanation: You can draw infinite circles of different sizes passing through a single point.

questions.text: How many circles can pass through two given points?
questions.explanation: Infinite circles can have their centers on the perpendicular bisector of the segment joining the two points.

questions.text: ABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral. If angle A is 80°, then angle C is:
questions.explanation: Opposite angles sum to 180°. 180 - 80 = 100°.

questions.text: If two chords AB and CD of a circle are equal, and the distance of AB from center is 4 cm, then distance of CD from center is:
questions.explanation: Equal chords are equidistant from the center.

questions.text: Angle subtended by major arc at center is:
questions.explanation: A major arc is more than a semi-circle, so its central angle is reflex (> 180°).

questions.text: If chord AB subtends 60° at center, then triangle OAB is:
questions.explanation: OA=OB (radii). If angle O is 60, then other two are (180-60)/2 = 60 each.

questions.text: A line segment joining any two points on a circle is called:
questions.explanation: Chords have both endpoints on the circle.

questions.text: In a cyclic quadrilateral, if all angles are equal, then it is a:
questions.explanation: A quadrilateral with all angles 90° and vertices on a circle is a rectangle.

questions.text: A sector is the region between an arc and ______.
questions.explanation: A sector is bounded by an arc and two radii.

questions.text: Value of pi (π) is the ratio of circumference to:
questions.explanation: π = C / d.

questions.text: Is every diameter a chord?
options: Only if it passes through center
questions.explanation: A chord joins two points on the circle; a diameter does exactly this while passing through the center.

questions.text: The angle subtended by a chord of length equal to radius at the center is:
questions.explanation: It forms an equilateral triangle OAB (OA=OB=AB).

questions.text: A cyclic parallelogram is a:
questions.explanation: In a parallelogram, opposite angles are equal. In cyclic quad, they add to 180. x+x=180 => x=90. Thus it's a rectangle.

questions.text: Angle in a major segment is always:
questions.explanation: Since a major segment corresponds to a minor arc, and angle at center is < 180, angle at circumference is < 90.

questions.text: Angle in a minor segment is always:
questions.explanation: Since a minor segment corresponds to a major arc (> 180°), half of that central angle is > 90°.
